blocks.content: June settled in easy. They fell back into each other the way they always had.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You're staring.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm not staring. I'm sitting in the same room as you.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: With your eyes pointed directly at me.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You're in the middle of the room. It's not intentional.
They were in his living room. His mom was somewhere upstairs. The television was on and neither of them was watching it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Do you want to watch something.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm fine.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You've said three words in twenty minutes.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm thinking.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: About.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: How it's the same. Being here with you. Except it isn't.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Is that a bad thing.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: No. It's a good thing. It's just strange — in the best way. I keep expecting it to feel different and it doesn't. And that feels different.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: That sentence made sense.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You understood it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah I did.
He reached over and took her hand. She looked down at it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We're going to be okay.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: We're going to be better than okay.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Don't get ahead of yourself.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I've been waiting since we were twelve.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Eleven. Don't rewrite history.
He laughed. She smiled at the ceiling. Outside, the yard was humming and warm.
